title: Listening Sample Test 1
instructions: Click on each of the mp3 players below. Then choose the best response to the questions or statements you hear. Please allow yourself 10 seconds after each statement to choose your answers.

questions.transcription: Will you take care of the kids while I'm away?
Do you have something up your sleeve?
options: No, I wouldn't hide anything from you.

questions.transcription: How do you earn a living?
Will she heed your advice?
I told them to keep it down.
options: They were throwing things out again.
They were making too much noise.

questions.transcription: She really gets a kick out of collecting stamps.
Her bank account is always in the red.
options: She's better off than she used to be.

title: Listening Sample Test 2
instructions: Click on each of the mp3 players below. Then choose the best response to the questions or statements you hear.

questions.transcription: Could you give me a lift?
options: No, I have a bad back.

questions.transcription: Were you able to find an empty space in the parking lot?
options: Yeah, it was pretty tough but I managed.

questions.transcription: Have you ever been there before?
My brother is an attorney in Seattle.
options: He has turned over a new leaf.

questions.transcription: I bought a round-trip ticket to Oklahoma City.
options: She is going to stay there.

questions.transcription: The elevator is broken again.
options: She will have to take the stairs.

title: Listening Sample Test 3
instructions: Listen to the conversations and choose the best response.

questions.transcription: -Your chances of getting into law school are slim. -Don't rain on my parade!
options: He's telling her not to discourage him.
He has changed the subject to the weather.

questions.transcription: -What's your opinion of Martha? -Can I be frank?
options: They have a mutual friend named Frank.

questions.transcription: -Any news from John? -Not yet, but I'll keep you posted.
options: He will send a letter to John.
He has received a letter from John.
He will tell her when he has heard.

questions.transcription: -I thought you said to meet in front of the restaurant...
I told you Mary's sister was coming...
The professor said that the exam was on Friday...

title: Listening Sample Test 4 - Lecture
instructions: Listen to the lecture and answer the questions.
lectureText: A group of English separatists known as the pilgrims first left England to live in Amsterdam in 1608. After spending a few years in their new city, though, many members of the group felt that they did not have enough independence. So in 1617, the pilgrims decided to leave Amsterdam to immigrate to America. However, many of these separatists were poor farmers who did not have much education or social status, and, not surprisingly, the group had many financial problems that prevented them from beginning their journey. Although their inability to finance themselves caused many disputes and disagreements, the pilgrims finally managed to obtain financing from a well-known and affluent London businessman named Thomas Weston.

questions.transcription: When did the first Pilgrims leave England?
What prevented the Pilgrims from beginning their journey to America?
